statement: V0 should use provider-aware output reservation rules instead of one generic bounded heuristic: OpenAI PAYG `total_tokens` reserves estimated input plus the full effective output cap, Anthropic `output_tokens` reserves full `max_tokens`, and Gemini v0 makes no output-side shared-bucket reservation because Gemini's documented quota shape does not require one.
rationale: A single generic heuristic would either under-reserve for Anthropic or overfit the design to one provider. Reserving according to provider-documented quota semantics keeps the shared limiter conservative and honest while still allowing reconciliation after completion.
keyAssumptions: The worker can compute an effective requested output cap before provider submission, either from caller input or engine policy defaults
The team prefers quota correctness over throughput-maximizing but under-documented heuristics
Gemini output usage can remain tracked-only until a documented enforced output bucket or stronger operational need appears
validationCriteria: Anthropic reservations never use a lower preflight output reservation than `max_tokens` for OTPM-governed calls
OpenAI PAYG `total_tokens` reservations include the full effective output cap rather than a guessed average output expectation
The policy remains compatible with reconciliation to actual usage and future registry overrides
critiquePoints: Full-cap reservations may reduce short-term throughput for workloads with systematically oversized output caps
Provider-plan-specific variants may eventually justify more nuanced reservation policies
questions: Should the engine keep one shared `reserved_output_budget` field in the attempt ledger even when the enforced quota dimension is `total_tokens` rather than `output_tokens`?
